initiative: US Senate Banking Committee Expected to Hold a Formal Vote on CLARITY Act as Early as Next Week
what_happened: The Senate Banking Committee is preparing to notice a markup for the Digital Asset Market CLARITY Act during the week of May 11, with draft legislative text already circulated to select industry participants ahead of a potential Thursday committee vote. initiative: US jobless claims come in at 200,000; strong labor data keeps pressure on crypto
what_happened: Initial jobless claims for the week ending May 2 printed at 200,000, coming in below the 205,000 consensus and just above the prior week's revised 190,000 figure — itself the lowest reading since 1969. The data, released by the Labor Department, reinforces a persistently tight employment backdrop that reduces the Federal Reserve's urgency to cut rates, sustaining elevated real yields and a firmer dollar that weigh on risk assets. For crypto markets specifically, the transmission mechanism is well-established: higher-for-longer rate expectations lift Treasury yields, compress liquidity-driven demand for high-beta assets like Bitcoin and Ethereum, and reduce the probability of the kind of aggressive easing cycle that has historically catalyzed sustained digital asset rallies.

initiative: US spot bitcoin ETFs draw $532 million as inflows extend to a third day, led by BlackRock and Fidelity
what_happened: US Spot Bitcoin ETFs Register $532M in Net Inflows on Third Consecutive Positive Day On May 4, 2026, the 13 SEC-registered spot bitcoin ETFs collectively absorbed $532.2 million in net capital, bringing the three-day running total to $1.18 billion and lifting cumulative net inflows across the product category to $59.3 billion since launch. BlackRock's IBIT and Fidelity's FBTC dominated activity, contributing $335.5 million and $184.6 million respectively, with Morgan Stanley's MSBT adding a further $12.2 million — the remaining ten funds, including Grayscale's GBTC, reported flat flows and zero outflows.

initiative: CFTC looks to cement non-custodial software developer protections following Phantom no-action letter
what_happened: CFTC Moves to Formalize Non-Custodial Developer Exemptions via Rulemaking CFTC Chair Michael Selig announced at Consensus Miami that the agency intends to convert its March 2026 no-action relief granted to Phantom wallet into binding rules, establishing a permanent regulatory safe harbor for non-custodial software developers who meet specified conditions and do not hold customer assets. The March no-action letter had already signaled that such developers would not be required to register as introducing brokers under the Commodity Exchange Act, but Selig indicated a preference for codified rulemaking over ad hoc guidance to provide durable legal certainty.

initiative: CME Group to launch cash-settled bitcoin volatility futures
what_happened: CME Group announced it will list Bitcoin Volatility futures (ticker: BVI) on June 1, 2026, pending CFTC regulatory approval, making it the first such product available on a regulated U.S. exchange. The cash-settled contracts will carry a notional multiplier of $500 times the CME CF Bitcoin Volatility Index (BVX), which measures 30-day implied volatility derived in real time from CME's existing CFTC-regulated Bitcoin and Bitcoin Micro options order books — infrastructure that CF Benchmarks and CME jointly formalized in December 2024.

initiative: Crypto's value is from being outside regulatory apparatus, says Arthur Hayes
what_happened: Speaking at Consensus Miami 2026, Arthur Hayes — co-founder of BitMEX and CIO of the crypto-focused family office Maelstrom — argued that legislative and regulatory integration efforts such as the Senate's Clarity Act are structurally misaligned with bitcoin's core value driver, which he identifies solely as the rate of global fiat money creation. Drawing on a cross-administration price history, Hayes pointed to specific monetary expansion events — post-2008 bank bailouts, COVID stimulus, and Ukraine-driven commodity inflation — as the operative catalysts behind bitcoin's appreciation, rather than any policy legitimacy conferred by regulatory frameworks. His remarks arrive at a moment of active U.S.

initiative: Crypto ETPs log five straight weeks of inflows, topping $4B
what_happened: CoinShares' weekly flow report shows global crypto ETP products recorded $117.8 million in net inflows for the most recent week, but the aggregate figure obscures a pronounced intraweek dislocation: Monday through Thursday generated $619 million in net outflows before a single-day $737 million surge on Friday rescued the weekly balance. Bitcoin-linked products, predominantly U.S. spot ETFs operating under the SEC's post-approval regulatory framework, absorbed $192.1 million in net inflows, while Ethereum products shed $81.6 million as traders unwound ETH exposure despite that asset class having posted strong inflows in prior weeks. initiative: Global crypto ETP inflow streak extends to five weeks, masking sharp intraweek reversal: CoinShares
what_happened: CoinShares' weekly flow report showed global crypto exchange-traded products attracted $117.8 million in net inflows for the week ending May 2, extending a consecutive positive-flow streak to five weeks and accumulating over $4 billion in that period, though the headline figure conceals significant underlying stress. Products run by issuers including BlackRock, Fidelity, Grayscale, Bitwise, and 21Shares experienced four straight days of outflows totalling $619 million Monday through Thursday, before a single Friday session reversed the trajectory with $737 million in inflows — one of the largest single-day figures recorded in 2026.

initiative: Coinbase cuts 14% of staff as AI reshapes how crypto companies operate
what_happened: Coinbase announced the elimination of approximately 660 roles — roughly 14% of its 4,700-person workforce — with CEO Brian Armstrong attributing the decision to both a sustained crypto market downturn and a structural shift in engineering productivity driven by AI tooling that has materially reduced headcount requirements for software development. The move is part of a broader cost restructuring at the Nasdaq-listed exchange intended to right-size the business through the current down-market cycle rather than carry overhead built for more favorable conditions.

initiative: Coinbase to cut 14% of workforce as Armstrong pushes AI-native structure
what_happened: Coinbase announced on May 5, 2026, that it is eliminating approximately 14% of its global workforce, with affected employees losing system access the same day as a security precaution given the firm's custodial obligations. CEO Brian Armstrong framed the reduction as a response to both cyclical softness in crypto markets and a structural reorganization around AI-driven workflows, citing the ability of smaller, automated teams to compress development timelines significantly. The restructuring includes flattening the organization to a maximum of five management layers below the CEO and COO, eliminating pure management roles in favor of player-coach positions, and piloting AI-native pods where single individuals may span product, design, and engineering functions.

initiative: Coinbase opens crypto access for Australia’s self-managed retirement funds
what_happened: Coinbase Australia has activated dedicated account support for self-managed superannuation funds (SMSFs) under its Australian Financial Services Licence, secured in April 2026, which subjects the firm to the same conduct, disclosure, and consumer protection obligations as traditional financial intermediaries. The product includes ATO-aligned downloadable transaction data and a verification workflow tailored to the trustee structure of SMSFs — addressing a key operational friction point for the roughly 664,000 funds collectively holding AU$1.06 trillion in assets. The move places Coinbase in direct competition with OKX, which launched a comparable SMSF-focused crypto platform in September 2025 featuring custody, compliance tooling, and end-of-year reporting.

initiative: Coinbase Australia launches crypto support for self-directed retirement funds
what_happened: Coinbase Australia has formally extended its platform to serve self-managed superannuation fund (SMSF) trustees, a move enabled by the Australian Financial Services Licence the exchange secured just weeks prior. The offering includes ATO-aligned accounting data exports and an entity verification workflow adapted to SMSF legal structures, addressing the specific compliance and audit requirements that have historically made institutional-grade crypto access difficult for this vehicle. SMSFs are regulated directly by the Australian Taxation Office and collectively held approximately AU$1.06 trillion across roughly 664,000 funds at end-2025, representing a substantial addressable pool of retirement capital that is already legally permitted to hold crypto.

initiative: Self-directed investors power bitcoin ETF launch despite Morgan Stanley’s scale
what_happened: Morgan Stanley's spot bitcoin ETF (MSBT) gathered over $200 million in assets within its first few weeks, a notably fast pace for a traditional ETF launch, but the inflows were driven almost entirely by self-directed retail clients making autonomous allocation decisions rather than through the firm's financial advisor channel. Amy Oldenburg, Morgan Stanley's newly appointed head of digital assets, disclosed at Consensus Miami that a meaningful portion of demand is coming from existing spot crypto holders migrating assets from self-custodied wallets into regulated exchange-traded products, signaling a structural shift in how crypto-native investors interface with traditional financial infrastructure.

initiative: Ripple’s David Schwartz reveals he is nearly all in on XRP
what_happened: Ripple CTO Emeritus David Schwartz disclosed on May 5, 2026, that his personal crypto holdings have been reduced to almost exclusively XRP and Ripple equity, with negligible exposure to any other digital asset. Speaking in an online discussion, he characterized the concentration as largely unplanned rather than a deliberate strategic allocation, which is notable given his decades-long technical leadership at the firm that issues XRP. Schwartz simultaneously argued that broad sector diversification remains a rational approach for investors who lack conviction on which specific assets will win — a framing that separates his own insider-adjacent position from a general endorsement of XRP concentration.

initiative: Standard Chartered expands further into crypto with stake in GSR at $1 billion valuation
what_happened: SC Ventures, the venture capital arm of Standard Chartered, has taken a minority equity stake in GSR — a crypto market-making and liquidity firm founded in 2013 by former Goldman Sachs traders — at a valuation exceeding $1 billion, representing the first external investment in GSR's thirteen-year history. The deal is structured as a broader commercial partnership, with the two firms intending to co-develop regulated market infrastructure connecting traditional banking rails to digital asset markets, with a particular focus on tokenization services for institutional clients.

initiative: CFTC prediction market rules spark industry debate
what_happened: The CFTC's comment period closed on a March 12 proposed rulemaking that would formalize federal oversight of prediction market event contracts under Part 38 of the Commodity Exchange Act, drawing over 1,500 submissions from a notably divided set of stakeholders. Major platform operators Kalshi and Polymarket, alongside venture firm Andreessen Horowitz, submitted letters explicitly backing CFTC's exclusive jurisdiction, arguing that fragmented state-level intervention would undermine compliance frameworks already built around federal oversight.

initiative: Chainlink price spikes 3% at Consensus 2026
what_happened: LINK posted its largest single-session advance in two weeks on May 4, breaking out of a month-long consolidation range between roughly $8.70 and $9.58, coinciding with the opening of the Consensus 2026 conference in Miami and Bitcoin reclaiming the $80,000 level in a broad risk-on session. The move was preceded by a notable exchange outflow signal, with Santiment data recording 970,430 LINK tokens leaving centralized venues on April 27—the highest single-day outflow since December 2025—suggesting accumulation pressure had been building ahead of the catalyst.

initiative: Hut 8 swaps Coinbase loan for cheaper FalconX deal, slashing borrowing costs as it bets big on AI
what_happened: Hut 8 has replaced its existing Coinbase Credit bitcoin-backed lending arrangement with a new $200 million, 364-day facility provided by crypto prime broker FalconX, reducing its fixed interest rate from 9% to 7% — a 200-basis-point improvement that directly lowers the company's cost of capital. A material operational consequence of the refinancing is the release of approximately 3,300 BTC (~$260 million) that had been locked as pledged collateral under the prior facility's covenants, restoring the company's ability to deploy or leverage that bitcoin more flexibly.

initiative: Coinbase boosts Solana trading with DFlow integration
what_happened: Coinbase has designated DFlow as its primary liquidity routing protocol for Solana-based spot and prediction market trading, replacing an infrastructure stack that previously failed to route approximately one in every 30 trades due to insufficient liquidity coverage. Under the new arrangement, that failure rate drops to roughly one in 250, while tokens that previously returned no executable sell-side quotes are now routable through DFlow's aggregation logic, which sources paths that competing aggregators do not surface. DFlow already processes order flow for over one million active traders monthly and was previously integrated by prediction market operator Kalshi in December 2025, giving it an established track record in high-throughput, latency-sensitive environments.

initiative: Coinbase urges CFTC to keep prediction markets under rules
what_happened: Coinbase submitted a formal letter on April 30 to CFTC Secretary Christopher Kirkpatrick in response to the agency's Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking, arguing that prediction market products already fall within the CFTC's existing statutory authority and require no new legislative mandate to regulate. Chief Policy Officer Faryar Shirzad framed event-based contracts as functionally analogous to traditional futures instruments, both serving as mechanisms for aggregating dispersed information into price signals, and called for a principles-based oversight framework prioritizing market integrity.

initiative: Morgan Stanley advises 2%–4% Bitcoin exposure as demand grows
what_happened: Morgan Stanley's head of digital asset strategy, Amy Oldenburg, publicly outlined a 2%–4% Bitcoin portfolio allocation recommendation for clients during the Bitcoin Conference in Las Vegas, framing it as a response to growing client interest in regulated crypto access rather than speculative positioning. The bank's Bitcoin ETP, MSBT, validated that demand early, pulling in over $100 million within six days of launch entirely from self-directed clients before the product was even made available through Morgan Stanley's advisory channel — highlighting a structural lag between retail initiative and adviser-driven distribution.

initiative: Morgan Stanley's Oldenburg: Bitcoin on U.S. bank balance sheets is coming, just not yet
what_happened: Morgan Stanley's head of digital asset strategy, Amy Oldenburg, publicly acknowledged that U.S. bank balance sheet holdings of bitcoin are a foreseeable outcome but flagged a multi-layered regulatory gauntlet — Federal Reserve oversight, Basel III/IV capital treatment, and coordination across global supervisors — as the substantive barriers preventing near-term action at a firm of Morgan Stanley's systemic scale. Her comments came alongside disclosure that the bank's newly launched MSBT bitcoin ETP, custodied jointly by Coinbase and BNY Mellon, gathered over $100 million in its first six days exclusively through self-directed retail channels, before the product was even made available to the firm's own financial advisor network.

initiative: SEC schedules CLARITY Act roundtable in May as Senate markup targets week of May 11
what_happened: The SEC convened a formal roundtable with CFTC officials and crypto industry representatives to work through the core jurisdictional question under the CLARITY Act — specifically, which digital assets fall under SEC versus CFTC oversight — building directly on the joint taxonomy the two agencies published on March 17 that designated 16 assets as commodities. The event is timed to precede the Senate Banking Committee's targeted markup during the week of May 11, effectively serving as a public signal from the regulator that it is operationally prepared to administer the legislation. That markup window is compressed to roughly eight working days before the May 21 Memorial Day recess, after an April attempt was displaced by the Warsh confirmation process.

initiative: A16z backs CFTC, says state-by-state rules on prediction markets create ‘barrier to impartial access’
what_happened: Andreessen Horowitz submitted an 18-page comment letter to the CFTC arguing that state-level enforcement actions — including cease-and-desist orders and proposed bans issued by regulators in Illinois, Arizona, Connecticut, New York, and Wisconsin — undermine the commission's exclusive federal jurisdiction over event contracts classified as swaps. The firm contended that compelling exchanges to geo-block users by state of residence would fragment liquidity and violate CFTC fair-access requirements, effectively disadvantaging retail and institutional participants in states pursuing their own licensing or prohibition regimes.

initiative: CaixaBank AI agent to be first port of call for customer interactions
what_happened: CaixaBank is placing an artificial intelligence agent as the first point of contact for customers who start a digital conversation to request information about any of the bank's products.

initiative: European crypto asset manager CoinShares records $7.4 billion AUM in first annual filing since Nasdaq listing
what_happened: CoinShares filed its first U.S. annual report following the completion of a $1.2 billion SPAC merger with Vine Hill, through which it began trading on Nasdaq under the ticker CSHR earlier in May 2026, adding a U.S. listing to its existing European public status. The filing disclosed $7.4 billion in gross AUM and $165.7 million in total full-year revenue for 2025, with asset management revenue growing 13% year-on-year to $126.4 million, while reported net income declined to $114.3 million largely due to the absence of a one-time $36.8 million FTX claims gain that inflated 2024 figures.

initiative: Australian regulator warns of ungoverned AI risks to banks
what_happened: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (Apra) has called for a step-change in how banks, insurers and superannuation trustees manage AI-related risks as the technology continues to rapidly evolve.

initiative: CommBank builds AI agent that spots fraud and helps build defences
what_happened: Commonwealth Bank of Australia has deployed an agentic AI system designed to help detect emerging fraud and scam patterns in transaction and payments data and generate the rules needed to help intercept them.

initiative: Scope of Insurers Subject to the Recovery and Resolution Planning Requirements in the FSB Key Attributes: Final Report
what_happened: The Financial Stability Board has published its final guidance establishing a structured framework for national authorities to determine which insurers must be subject to recovery and resolution planning requirements under the FSB's Key Attributes of Effective Resolution Regimes. The report follows the FSB's 2022 decision to discontinue the annual designation of global systemically important insurers, replacing that binary G-SII identification process with a more flexible, criteria-based scoping methodology that individual jurisdictions apply using their own supervisory judgment and market context.

initiative: Scope of Insurers Subject to Recovery and Resolution Planning Requirements in the Key Attributes: Overview of consultation responses
what_happened: The FSB received nine responses to its November 2025 consultation on criteria for determining which insurers should be subject to recovery and resolution planning requirements under the Key Attributes framework, with the consultation period closing in February 2026. Respondents — drawn from both public authorities and private sector participants — broadly supported the proposed scoping criteria but flagged concerns around regulatory overreach and potential duplication with existing national or sectoral frameworks. The FSB has now published both an overview of those responses and a final report incorporating targeted revisions to address the feedback, alongside updated guidance on identifying critical functions for insurers.

initiative: Identification of Critical Functions of Insurers: Practices paper – Revised version
what_happened: The Financial Stability Board has released a revised version of its 2023 practices paper on identifying critical functions of insurers, updated to reflect a new definition of "critical function" introduced in the FSB's concurrently finalised guidance on the scope of insurers subject to recovery and resolution planning under the Key Attributes framework. The revision draws on approaches from four jurisdictions — Australia, China, France, and the Netherlands — documenting how each has developed or advanced methodology for scoping which insurer functions are sufficiently vital to the real economy that their disruption would warrant resolution intervention.

initiative: Key Attributes Assessment Methodology for the Insurance Sector: Revised version
what_happened: The Financial Stability Board published a revised version of its Key Attributes Assessment Methodology for the Insurance Sector on 29 April 2026, updating the 2020 original to incorporate changes stemming from newly finalized guidance on which insurers fall within the scope of recovery and resolution planning requirements. The revision reflects the FSB's 2022 decision—reaffirmed in November 2025—to discontinue annual identification of global systemically important insurers (G-SIIs) and instead rely on the IAIS Holistic Framework to assess systemic risk, a structural shift that necessitated an updated definition of "critical function" embedded in the methodology via an explanatory note.

initiative: Developing Effective Resolution Strategies and Plans for Systemically Important Insurers: Revised version
what_happened: The Financial Stability Board has released a revised version of its 2016 guidance on resolution strategies and plans for systemically important insurers, updated to incorporate changes to the definition of "critical functions" introduced through a companion scoping paper finalized simultaneously. The revised guidance is directed at national resolution authorities and Crisis Management Groups overseeing global systemically important insurers, setting out how preferred resolution strategies should be determined by analyzing business models, policyholder protection arrangements, and the criticality of specific insurance functions.

initiative: Investment funds' de facto currency risk exposure
what_happened: Investment Funds' De Facto Currency Risk Exposure A BIS research bulletin published in April 2026 introduces a return-sensitivity framework to measure the actual, rather than declared, currency hedge ratios of investment funds, finding materially different behaviour between asset classes. Bond funds maintain consistently high hedge ratios, though these show some responsiveness to the cost of hedging, while equity funds exhibit volatile, opportunistic positioning that functionally resembles active currency speculation rather than systematic risk management.

initiative: Bitcoin tops $79,000 as crypto rally gathers steam; Circle, Coinbase, Strategy lead
what_happened: Bitcoin reached an 11-week high above $79,000 on April 22, driven by a combination of improving macro sentiment following President Trump's Iran ceasefire extension and technical positioning dynamics in derivatives markets. Perpetual swap funding rates fell to near three-year lows while open interest simultaneously rose, a configuration K33 Research's Vetle Lunde flagged as indicative of concentrated short buildup in leveraged positions — conditions that historically precede a mechanically driven short squeeze. Crypto-linked equities moved sharply in sympathy, with Strategy gaining 10% as its BTC-heavy balance sheet amplified the move, while Circle Internet and Coinbase rose 9% and 6% respectively ahead of Circle's anticipated public listing.

initiative: The London P2P sweep: UK FCA raids eight illegal peer-to-peer trading hubs
what_happened: The FCA, operating jointly with HMRC and the South West Regional Organised Crime Unit, executed simultaneous raids across eight London locations suspected of running unregistered crypto peer-to-peer trading operations — marking the regulator's first coordinated enforcement action of this type. Each site received a cease-and-desist notice, and seized evidence is now underpinning multiple active criminal investigations focused on money laundering exposure. The action carries particular regulatory weight given that the FCA has confirmed zero registered P2P crypto traders currently exist in the UK, meaning all such operations are by definition unlawful under the existing AML registration framework.

initiative: UK FCA carries out first crackdown on illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading
what_happened: The FCA conducted coordinated physical inspections of eight London premises suspected of operating unregistered peer-to-peer crypto trading activity, working alongside HM Revenue & Customs and the South West Regional Organised Crime Unit. Cease-and-desist letters were issued at each site, and evidence gathered is now feeding into active criminal investigations — a notable escalation given that the regulator has confirmed zero FCA-registered peer-to-peer crypto traders or platforms currently exist in the UK. The action is grounded in existing AML registration requirements that already apply to certain crypto activities ahead of the broader regulatory regime expected to go live in October 2027, meaning firms cannot defer compliance on the basis that the full framework is incomplete.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of First Financial Bank
what_happened: Federal Reserve Enforcement Action Against Former First Financial Bank Employee The Federal Reserve Board issued a consent prohibition order on April 22, 2026, against Destiny Lara, a former employee of First Financial Bank, headquartered in Abilene, Texas. The action stems from findings of breach of fiduciary duty and bribery, two serious violations that typically indicate insider misconduct involving the misuse of a position of trust for personal gain. Consent prohibition orders bar the named individual from participating in the affairs of any federally regulated financial institution, making this a career-ending sanction in the banking sector.

initiative: Crypto giant GSR launches its first ETF to give investors an easy way to bet on the big 3 tokens
what_happened: GSR, a firm best known as a crypto liquidity provider and OTC market maker, has entered the asset management space by listing the GSR Crypto Core3 ETF (BESO) on Nasdaq—its first registered investment product. The actively managed fund holds bitcoin, ether, and solana, rebalances weekly using proprietary research signals, and can generate staking yield on eligible positions, with Framework Digital Advisors serving as the named investment adviser and a 1% annual management fee applying. The structure is notable because it combines active allocation with staking income in a single wrapper, features that have only recently become permissible in U.S.-listed crypto ETFs following regulatory shifts that allowed in-kind treatment and yield-bearing mechanics.

initiative: SEC confirms exemption for directors and officers of EEA Foreign Private Issuers
what_happened: The SEC has granted an exemption relieving directors and officers of EEA-domiciled foreign private issuers from the beneficial ownership and transaction reporting obligations under Section 16(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, which would otherwise require prompt disclosure of changes in holdings of registered equity securities. The decision reflects a regulatory equivalence determination, with the SEC acknowledging that EU Market Abuse Regulation (MAR) already imposes materially comparable disclosure obligations on persons discharging managerial responsibilities at listed companies.

initiative: Strategy overtakes BlackRock IBIT in bitcoin holdings after bear market buying
what_happened: Strategy Reclaims Top Bitcoin Holder Position from BlackRock IBIT Strategy has accumulated approximately 80,000 BTC in 2026 alone — largely during a period when bitcoin fell more than 50% from its October all-time high — pushing its total holdings to 815,061 BTC and edging past BlackRock's IBIT, which holds 802,824 BTC. The accumulation was funded primarily through a combination of at-the-market equity issuance, convertible debt, and perpetual preferred securities (STRC), the last of which has become a scalable, recurring capital source that distinguishes Strategy's model from passive ETF structures.

initiative: European banks are at risk of losing customers to rivals with better crypto tools
what_happened: A Boerse Stuttgart Digital survey of 6,000 retail investors across Germany, Italy, Spain, and France—conducted between August 2025 and January 2026—found that 35% would consider switching their primary bank to one offering stronger crypto investment services, with Spain leading at 40%. Approximately 25% of respondents already hold digital assets, yet over 60% report feeling poorly informed and 76% cite insufficient regulation as a primary risk concern, underscoring a gap between adoption and confidence. Critically, surveyed investors were more than twice as likely to trust their incumbent bank for crypto custody and trading than a specialist platform, meaning traditional institutions hold a structural trust advantage they have so far failed to monetize.

initiative: UK invites crypto giant Bybit to London to win over some of UAE’s innovation shine
what_happened: UK government-linked economic development officials formally invited Bybit CEO Ben Zhou to London during UK Fintech Week, arranging meetings with the Financial Conduct Authority and members of the House of Lords to discuss the country's forthcoming crypto regulatory framework and potential establishment of a Bybit presence in the jurisdiction. The outreach is explicitly framed around competitive positioning against the UAE, which has attracted both Bybit—headquartered in Dubai since 2022—and Binance, which relocated there in 2025, creating a cluster effect that drew smaller crypto firms to the region.

initiative: ESMA support ESEF implementation with updated taxonomy
what_happened: ESMA published the 2025 ESEF XBRL taxonomy files and an updated Conformance Suite on 21 April 2026, providing the technical infrastructure EU-listed issuers and their software vendors need to prepare IFRS consolidated financial statements in structured electronic format for the 2026 reporting cycle. The key substantive change embedded in the taxonomy is the incorporation of IFRS 18 Presentation and Disclosure in Financial Statements — mandatory from 1 January 2027 but permitting early adoption — with two separate entry points allowing filers to report under either the existing IAS 1 framework or the incoming IFRS 18 standard without requiring an RTS amendment.

initiative: Grayscale’s amended Hyperliquid ETF filing drops Coinbase as custodian, names Anchorage
what_happened: Grayscale submitted an amended S-1 filing with the SEC for its proposed Hyperliquid ETF (ticker: GHYP), replacing Coinbase with Anchorage Digital Bank as the fund's custodian — a notable counterparty shift given Coinbase Custody Trust Company's dominant position across nearly all existing U.S. spot crypto ETFs. Anchorage, the first federally chartered crypto bank in the U.S. and recently valued at $4.2 billion following a $100 million Tether equity investment, had previously served only as a secondary custodian for Grayscale's Bitcoin and Ethereum trusts, making this a meaningful elevation of the relationship.

initiative: U.S. crypto adoption is rebounding, bitcoin still dominates, Deutsche Bank says
what_happened: Deutsche Bank published findings from a 3,400-person retail survey across the U.S., U.K., and EU showing U.S. crypto participation rebounded to 12% in March 2026 from a February trough of 7%, recovering to levels last seen in mid-2025, though the survey's all-time high remains just 14% since tracking began in 2023. The recovery coincided with approximately $1.3 billion in net inflows into U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs during March, suggesting institutional and retail demand are moving in parallel after a soft start to the year. Despite the participation rebound, consumer price sentiment is bearish, with a majority of U.S. respondents expecting bitcoin to trade below current ~$75,000 levels by year-end and only 3% anticipating a return to the late-2025 peak above $120,000.

initiative: Nomura study says 65% of institutional investors see crypto as a vital portfolio diversifier
what_happened: Nomura and its crypto subsidiary Laser Digital surveyed more than 500 investment professionals in Japan, finding a measurable improvement in institutional sentiment toward digital assets compared to their 2024 baseline. The shift is being underpinned by Japan's ongoing refinement of its crypto regulatory framework — covering asset classification, taxation, and investor protections — alongside the broader global expansion of regulated investment vehicles such as ETFs and tokenized funds, which have lowered the structural barriers to entry.

initiative: Congresswoman Biggs discloses six-figure BlackRock bitcoin ETF buy as BTC rebounds
what_happened: U.S. Representative Sheri Biggs filed a House periodic transaction report disclosing that her spouse purchased between $100,001 and $250,000 of BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) on March 4, 2026, through a professionally managed UBS account held under the W.S.B Trust. The disclosure was submitted on April 16—43 days after the trade—placing it within the STOCK Act's mandatory 45-day reporting window, a meaningful distinction from a prior IBIT purchase by Biggs that was filed late and drew congressional trading transparency criticism. The March 4 entry point coincided with a volatile session in which bitcoin moved from roughly $68,000 to above $72,000, and the position has since appreciated as BTC approached $78,000 by mid-April.

initiative: Circle hit with class action lawsuit over alleged inaction in $280 million Drift exploit
what_happened: Law firm Gibbs Mura filed a class action suit against Circle Internet Financial on behalf of Drift Protocol investors who suffered losses in the April 1 exploit, which saw approximately $280 million drained from the Solana-based decentralized exchange after an attacker introduced a malicious asset and removed withdrawal limits following a six-month infiltration. The plaintiffs allege Circle had both the technical capability and contractual authority to freeze the stolen USDC but failed to act during a roughly six-hour window in which over $230 million was bridged from Solana to Ethereum via Circle's own cross-chain transfer protocol.

initiative: Thailand eyes stricter checks on crypto firm backers
what_happened: Thailand SEC Proposes Expanded Major Shareholder Definition for Crypto and Securities Firms Thailand's Securities and Exchange Commission has opened a public consultation, closing April 22, on draft rules that would classify funding providers and financial backers of existing major shareholders as major shareholders themselves, thereby subjecting them to the same formal SEC approval requirements. The proposal follows March 2026 rule changes that tightened indirect ownership tracing through corporate chains and expanded the definition of controlling persons to include spouses, cohabitants, minor children, and parties acting in concert on voting rights, which took effect March 4 with a 180-day compliance window for operators to submit approval requests.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with Community Bankshares, Inc.
what_happened: Federal Reserve Issues Cease and Desist Order Against Community Bankshares, Inc. The Federal Reserve Board executed a formal Cease and Desist Order dated April 14, 2026 against Community Bankshares, Inc., a bank holding company headquartered in LaGrange, Georgia, with the action publicly announced two days later. A Cease and Desist Order is among the more serious enforcement tools available to the Fed, legally compelling the institution to halt specific unsafe or unsound practices and requiring affirmative corrective measures under threat of further penalty.

initiative: Euronet acquires PaynoPain
what_happened: Euronet Worldwide (NASDAQ: EEFT), a global leader in payment processing and cross-border transactions, today announced it has entered into an agreement to acquire PaynoPain, a Spanish fintech company recognized for its digital-first, online payment solutions serving businesses of all sizes—from SMEs to large enterprises—across industries including e-commerce, hospitality, microfinance, and marketplace platforms.

initiative: SEC seeks public comment on consolidated audit trail and other data sources
what_happened: The Securities and Exchange Commission today issued a concept release soliciting public comment in support of a comprehensive review of the Consolidated Audit Trail (CAT) and other audit trails and related data sources currently used in the regulation of U.S. securities markets.

initiative: Bitcoin capped at $75,000 as Nasdaq and S&P 500 fly to new record highs
what_happened: Bitcoin has been unable to sustain a break above the $75,000 level, with the most recent attempt on April 15 stalling at the same technical threshold that has capped price action for roughly two months, leaving the asset approximately 40% below its October 2025 peak. The divergence with U.S. equities is operationally notable: the Nasdaq closed above 24,000 for its 11th consecutive record session and the S&P 500 cleared 7,000, suggesting macro risk appetite is not the binding constraint on bitcoin's underperformance. Crypto-adjacent equities—Coinbase (+6.2%), Robinhood (+10%), and Strategy (+4.4%)—participated in the broader rally, indicating that institutional exposure is rotating into listed proxies rather than spot BTC.

initiative: FSB Chair warns of rising financial risks stemming from Middle East conflict
what_happened: FSB Chair Issues Pre-G20 Warning on Conflict-Driven Systemic Risk In a formal letter addressed to G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors ahead of their April 16 meeting, FSB Chair and Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey outlined how ongoing Middle East conflict is compounding pre-existing structural vulnerabilities across global financial markets. Bailey specifically flagged the risk of simultaneous crystallisation across three interconnected pressure points: leveraged government bond basis trades concentrated among a small number of non-bank funds, stretched asset valuations in sectors including AI, and deteriorating credit quality within opaque private credit markets where contagion risk is amplified by limited transparency.

initiative: Supervising DEI in insurance - Executive Summary
what_happened: IAIS Application Paper: DEI Integration into Insurance Supervision Frameworks The Financial Stability Institute has summarised a new IAIS Application Paper that formally embeds diversity, equity, and inclusion considerations into the supervisory assessment of insurer governance and risk management, operating under the existing Insurance Core Principles — specifically ICP 7 on corporate governance and ICP 8 on risk management and internal controls. The paper establishes that deficient DEI practices constitute a concrete prudential risk vector, with identified exposures spanning governance quality, legal liability, reputational standing, and talent retention, rather than treating DEI as a standalone conduct or social obligation.

initiative: Basel III liquidity indicators increase slightly while risk-based capital and leverage ratios are stable for large internationally active banks, latest Basel III monitoring exercise shows
what_happened: The Basel Committee on Banking Supervision released its semi-annual Basel III monitoring report based on bank data as of 30 June 2025, covering 150 institutions across 101 Group 1 (large internationally active) and 49 Group 2 (smaller) banks, including 29 G-SIBs. Liquidity Coverage Ratios and Net Stable Funding Ratios edged higher over the first half of 2025, while risk-based capital and leverage ratios held broadly flat, suggesting the sector absorbed the period without material balance sheet stress or capital erosion. The average Tier 1 minimum required capital impact under the fully phased-in framework narrowed for Group 1 banks, reflecting ongoing national implementation progress ahead of the 2028 deadline when transitional arrangements expire.

initiative: No-Action Letter – State Trust Companies as Crypto Custodians
what_happened: SEC Staff Issues No-Action Relief Allowing Crypto Custody at State Trust Companies The SEC's Division of Investment Management issued a no-action letter confirming it will not recommend enforcement action against registered investment advisers or regulated funds that custody crypto assets with state-chartered trust companies exercising fiduciary powers comparable to those of national banks. The relief resolves a longstanding ambiguity over whether such entities qualify as "banks" under the custody provisions of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 and the Investment Company Act of 1940—a definitional gap that had left advisers and funds uncertain about whether their chosen crypto custodian, often the only practically available one, was legally permissible.

initiative: Taiwan stocks surge as AI boom drives fastest growth since 1987
what_happened: Taiwan's TWSE Index posted a 22.7% monthly gain in April, reaching 38,926.63 and bringing its year-to-date advance to 34.4%, according to Bank of America data, as surging global orders for AI servers, advanced semiconductors, and cloud computing hardware translated directly into record export figures — March exports rose 61.8% year-over-year to an all-time monthly high of $80 billion. The rally is underpinned by concrete economic throughput: Q1 GDP expanded 13.7% year-over-year, the strongest reading in nearly four decades, with technology stocks now representing over four-fifths of the market's $4 trillion total capitalization and accounting for 79% of daily turnover, which itself averaged NT$1.23 trillion — up 187% year-over-year.

initiative: Kalshi confirms $1 billion raise at $22 billion valuation amid prediction market boom
what_happened: Kalshi closed a $1 billion Series F round led by Coatue, with participation from Sequoia Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Paradigm, IVP, Morgan Stanley, and ARK Invest, placing the CFTC-regulated event-contract exchange at a $22 billion post-money valuation. The capital will be deployed toward block trading infrastructure, broker integrations, and risk products targeting asset managers and insurance firms, reflecting a strategic push to deepen institutional market share as hedge funds and prop trading desks increasingly use event contracts alongside conventional derivatives.

initiative: Poonam Gupta: Inflation targeting in India - the past, the present and the future
what_happened: RBI Renews Inflation Targeting Mandate Through 2031, Deputy Governor Outlines Framework Assessment The Government of India formally renewed its inflation targeting mandate via Gazette notification on March 25, 2026, retaining the existing 4% target with a ±2% tolerance band for a further five years through March 2031, leaving the core framework architecture unchanged. RBI Deputy Governor Poonam Gupta used a joint NCAER seminar to publicly assess the decade-long track record of the framework, including the public consultation process and the four structural questions it examined, drawing on both domestic outcomes and international comparisons.

what_happened: Aakash Girimath Posted May 6, 2026 at 4:46 am EST. initiative: Kelp DAO moves rsETH to Chainlink CCIP as LayerZero dispute grows
what_happened: Kelp DAO announced it will migrate its rsETH liquid restaking token from LayerZero's OFT standard to Chainlink's Cross-Chain Token standard under CCIP, following an April 18 exploit in which attackers drained 116,500 rsETH—worth approximately $292 million—from its LayerZero-powered bridge by exploiting a single-DVN (decentralized verifier network) configuration. The two protocols are publicly disputing responsibility for that configuration: Kelp contends LayerZero's team was aware of and implicitly endorsed the 1-of-1 verifier setup during integration discussions dating to January 2024, while LayerZero CEO Bryan Pellegrino asserts Kelp itself downgraded from a default multi-DVN arrangement to the weaker single-verifier setup that LayerZero does not recommend for production.

initiative: Cybersecurity strains grow as AI challenges Australian banks
what_happened: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) issued a formal letter to regulated financial institutions — banks, insurers, and superannuation trustees — warning that existing cybersecurity frameworks are failing to keep pace with rapid advances in frontier AI models. APRA specifically identified advanced-coding-capable systems, citing Anthropic's Claude Mythos as an example of technology that could enable malicious actors to detect and exploit infrastructure vulnerabilities at scale. The regulator's supervisory review found that boards and senior leadership lack sufficient technical literacy to govern AI-related risks, and that heavy reliance on third-party AI vendors is creating material blind spots around potential misuse or manipulation of those tools.

initiative: Luis de Guindos: Presentation of the European Central Bank Annual Report 2025 to the Committee on Economic and Monetary Affairs of the European Parliament
what_happened: ECB Annual Report 2025 Presented to European Parliament; Rate Cycle Pause Confirmed ECB Vice-President Luis de Guindos delivered the institution's Annual Report for 2025 to the European Parliament's Committee on Economic and Monetary Affairs on 4 May 2026, marking the formal accountability exercise required under the ECB's institutional framework and his final such appearance before his mandate expires. The euro area recorded 1.4% GDP growth in 2025, with early momentum driven by export frontloading ahead of anticipated US tariffs before shifting to domestic demand, while headline inflation averaged 2.1%—effectively at the 2% target—enabling the Governing Council to cut the deposit facility rate by a cumulative 100 basis points to 2.0% by mid-2025, a level held unchanged since.

initiative: Kraken Partners With MoneyGram for Global Crypto-to-Cash Withdrawals, Says IPO Is “80% Ready”
what_happened: Kraken and MoneyGram have formalized a phased global distribution agreement enabling Kraken users to withdraw cryptocurrency as cash at approximately 500,000 MoneyGram retail agent locations across more than 100 countries, with support for hundreds of fiat currencies. The operational division of labor is clearly delineated: Kraken retains control of customer onboarding and KYC/AML obligations, while MoneyGram supplies the licensed money transmission infrastructure and regulatory coverage in each jurisdiction.

initiative: Santiago Bausili: Finance, economics and investments
what_happened: Santiago Bausili, President of the Banco Central de la República Argentina (BCRA), delivered a formal policy address at Expo EFI on 29 April 2026, providing a comprehensive operational update on Argentina's stabilization program now in its third year following its late-2023 launch. He reported that the BCRA had accumulated USD 6.9 billion in foreign reserve purchases year-to-date, materially exceeding market forecasts, and that the central bank had permitted over USD 1.3 billion in dividend repatriations by foreign corporates—the first such outflows permitted in six years—as exchange restrictions continue to be unwound in stages.

initiative: Aleš Michl: Forever hawkish
what_happened: Czech National Bank Governor Aleš Michl delivered a guest lecture at MIT Sloan School of Management on 30 April 2026, outlining the CNB's policy trajectory since he assumed the governorship in July 2022 amid 17.5% inflation that subsequently peaked at 18%. He detailed how a near-complete turnover of the seven-member Bank Board, driven by expiring term limits rather than political dismissal, allowed the new leadership to pursue a sustained disinflationary strategy despite inheriting a key rate already at 7%. The CNB reached its 2% inflation target in February 2024, ahead of most European peers, with inflation averaging 2.4–2.5% in the two years since.

initiative: Kelp DAO ditches LayerZero for Chainlink’s cross-chain infrastructure following $292 million exploit
what_happened: Following the April 18 exploit in which Lazarus Group-linked attackers drained 116,500 rsETH (~$292 million) by abusing a single-verifier LayerZero bridge configuration, Kelp DAO has migrated its cross-chain infrastructure to Chainlink's Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol (CCIP) and adopted the Cross-Chain Token (CCT) standard for rsETH. The attack exposed a structural weakness in LayerZero's default 1-of-1 Decentralized Verifier Network setup — a configuration later found to be running across roughly 47% of ~2,665 LayerZero applications — with the two parties publicly disputing whether that single-verifier default constituted a platform-level failure or a protocol-specific misconfiguration.

initiative: ESMA consults on a new simplified approach to updating MMF stress test parameters
what_happened: ESMA has opened a public consultation proposing to replace the existing practice of annually amending Section 5 of its MMF stress test Guidelines with a streamlined web-based publication of updated calibration parameters each year. Under the proposed structure, the Guidelines themselves would remain static as the governing framework and methodology, while a dedicated ESMA webpage would serve as the live, authoritative source for current calibration inputs — allowing funds to apply updates immediately upon approval rather than waiting for a formal guidelines amendment cycle.

initiative: It's transparency, not tech alone, that drives crypto adoption, panelists tell Consensus Miami
what_happened: At CoinDesk's Consensus Miami conference on May 5, senior product and finance executives from PayPal, Robinhood, Public.com, and 248 Ventures collectively argued that the primary constraint on retail crypto and AI-product uptake is user trust architecture rather than technical capability. Robinhood VP Nicola White disclosed that half of the platform's Q1 new users were first-time investors, using that data point to question whether 100x-leverage perpetuals should be available to retail customers at all — a direct operational critique of current industry product velocity following the October 2026 crypto liquidation event.

initiative: SEC Pumps the Brakes on First Prediction-Market ETFs, Delaying 24 Products
what_happened: The SEC intervened just before the automatic 75-day effectiveness deadline to halt the pending launches of 24 prediction-market ETFs filed in February by Roundhill Investments, GraniteShares, and Bitwise, requesting supplemental information on product structuring and investor risk disclosure. The proposed funds would have provided retail brokerage access to binary event contracts traded on CFTC-regulated venues such as Kalshi, covering macro and political outcomes, without requiring investors to hold accounts on those platforms directly. Bloomberg's ETF desk characterizes the hold as procedurally narrow—focused on pricing mechanics and real-time probability transparency—rather than a substantive rejection of the asset class.

initiative: Prediction markets under fire as insiders game the system
what_happened: A U.S. Army Special Forces soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, was charged by both the DOJ and CFTC on April 23 after allegedly exploiting advance knowledge of the U.S. military operation that captured Venezuelan former president Nicolás Maduro to place over $33,000 in bets on Polymarket, generating nearly $410,000 in winnings paid out in USDC. In response, Polymarket engaged Chainalysis to monitor for market integrity violations — a reactive compliance measure signaling that the platform's existing controls were insufficient to detect or prevent insider exploitation.

initiative: SEC pumps the brakes on prediction market ETFs set to debut this week: Reuters
what_happened: The SEC intervened to halt the launch of 24 prediction market ETFs from issuers including Bitwise, Roundhill, and GraniteShares that had been progressing through a 75-day expedited review window established under last year's fast-track rules. The products were designed to offer binary, yes-or-no exposure to outcomes such as the 2028 U.S. election result, tech-sector layoffs, and recession probability — structures that differ fundamentally from conventional ETFs in that investors could lose substantially all capital if the underlying outcome resolves against them.

initiative: ESMA launches its sixth stress test exercise for Central Counterparties
what_happened: ESMA has formally initiated its sixth mandatory stress test of EU-authorised central counterparties, covering 16 institutions including all EU CCPs and two UK-based Tier 2 CCPs subject to EMIR's third-country recognition framework. The exercise is underpinned by an adverse macroeconomic scenario developed by the European Systemic Risk Board and spans four analytical components: a credit stress test, concentration risk analysis, reverse stress testing, and — for the first time — a Recovery and Resolution Component designed to quantify the aggregate impact on stakeholders if CCPs activate their resolution tools simultaneously. Data collection begins in early May 2025, with final results scheduled for publication in Q1 2027, after joint validation with National Competent Authorities.

initiative: ESMA consults on guidelines on endorsement under the ESG Ratings Regulation
what_happened: ESMA Opens Consultation on ESG Ratings Endorsement Guidelines ESMA has published a consultation paper setting out draft guidelines under Article 11 of the ESG Ratings Regulation, which governs how EU-established ESG rating providers may endorse ratings produced by non-EU entities for use within the European market. The draft guidelines focus specifically on the information requirements that applicants must submit when seeking to use the endorsement pathway, aiming to create a consistent supervisory standard across the regime. This matters operationally because without clear endorsement rules, EU-authorised providers relying on third-country ratings in their products face uncertainty about compliance obligations and application completeness.

initiative: Broadridge closes CQG acquisition
what_happened: Global Fintech leader Broadridge Financial Solutions, Inc. (NYSE: BR), announced today that it has completed its acquisition of CQG, a leading global provider of futures and options trading, execution management, and market connectivity.

initiative: OpenAI ends Microsoft exclusivity and can now sell its models on AWS and Google Cloud
what_happened: On April 27, Microsoft and OpenAI formally restructured their 2019 partnership agreement, converting Microsoft's cloud license over OpenAI's intellectual property from exclusive to non-exclusive through 2032, while preserving Azure's right of first deployment for new models. The restructuring was legally necessary to resolve an impending dispute triggered by OpenAI's February $50 billion investment agreement with Amazon, which had granted AWS exclusive third-party cloud distribution rights for OpenAI's enterprise agent platform Frontier — terms that directly contradicted Microsoft's existing exclusivity.

initiative: Rezolve Ai SQD token goes live on Revolut

initiative: CLARITY Act window slams shut after May 21
what_happened: The CLARITY Act, the primary U.S. federal digital-asset market structure bill with backing from over 120 industry signatories including Coinbase, Kraken, Circle, and Andreessen Horowitz, must complete five sequential legislative steps — committee markup, committee vote, a 60-vote Senate floor threshold, and dual reconciliation between the Senate Banking and Agriculture Committee versions and the July 2025 House text — before the Senate enters Memorial Day recess on May 21. The April markup window was lost because the Kevin Warsh Federal Reserve confirmation consumed the Banking Committee's full calendar, leaving roughly eight working days after the Senate returns May 11 for all remaining procedural steps.

initiative: Ripple investors turn to new profit opportunities, with SHRMiner offering returns of up to $57,000 per month
what_happened: This content is paid promotional material ("Partner Content") published by crypto.news on behalf of SHRMiner, a cloud mining and yield platform, and does not reflect an independent news development. The piece advertises tiered investment contracts — ranging from $100 to $50,000 — that purportedly generate fixed daily returns, with the platform claiming a maximum monthly return of approximately $57,000 under a portfolio model. No regulatory registration, audited financials, counterparty disclosures, or verifiable operational infrastructure are cited to substantiate these return figures.

initiative: Tiff Macklem: Release of the Monetary Policy Report
what_happened: Bank of Canada Holds Rate at 2.25% Amid Middle East-Driven Energy Shock At its April 29, 2026 decision, the Bank of Canada's Governing Council held the overnight policy rate steady at 2.25% — unchanged since October 2025 — as policymakers weighed competing pressures from an energy-driven inflation spike against softening domestic demand and trade headwinds from U.S. tariffs. The escalation of the Middle East conflict has driven global oil prices to approximately USD 90/barrel, pushing Canadian CPI from 1.8% in February to 2.4% in March, with the Bank projecting a peak near 3% in April before a return to the 2% target by early 2026, contingent on oil prices declining to around USD 75/barrel.

initiative: Aleš Michl: A central bank. And bitcoin.
what_happened: Czech National Bank Governor Aleš Michl delivered a formal speech at The Bitcoin Conference 2026 in Las Vegas, marking a notable instance of a sitting G10-adjacent central bank governor directly engaging a crypto-native audience at an industry event. The speech framed the CNB's post-2022 monetary tightening—which successfully reduced inflation from near 20% to the 2% target—as a philosophical foundation for evaluating Bitcoin alongside traditional reserve management principles. Michl positioned the CNB's approach as structurally hawkish and pro-saving, implicitly signaling openness to hard-asset or scarcity-based monetary concepts consistent with that framework.

initiative: Jorgovanka Tabaković: Brave old world
what_happened: At an event in Belgrade hosted by the National Bank of Serbia, Governor Jorgovanka Tabaković delivered remarks at the launch of the EBRD's *Transition Report: Brave Old World*, presented by EBRD Research Director Ralph De Haas alongside regional officials including former North Macedonia central bank Governor Dimitar Bogov. The speech used the report's demographic focus as a platform to articulate the NBS's institutional view that declining fertility, labour force contraction, and population ageing represent primary structural risks to GDP growth, fiscal sustainability, and inflation dynamics across both Serbia and the broader European transition region.

initiative: Chiara Scotti: From analysis to action - AI in financial markets
what_happened: The Bank of Italy's Deputy Governor Chiara Scotti delivered closing remarks at a trilateral event co-organized by the OECD, European Commission, and Banca d'Italia to present a joint report examining how artificial intelligence is being adopted across Italian financial markets. The report maps current AI deployment practices among Italian market participants, benchmarks them against cross-country experience, and identifies regulatory gaps where greater clarity, coordination, and supervisory capacity are needed.

initiative: Kalshi flags more insider trading cases, including politician who appeared on FBoy Island
what_happened: Kalshi, the CFTC-regulated prediction market exchange, publicly disclosed three additional insider trading enforcement actions against political candidates who placed bets on their own electoral outcomes — a practice the platform classifies as a direct violation of its rulebook given candidates' ability to influence contract results by choosing whether to enter or exit a race. The cases involved a Virginia U.S. Senate candidate, a Minnesota state lawmaker running for Congress, and a Texas congressional candidate, with penalties ranging from $540 to $6,229 in fines plus five-year trading suspensions and profit disgorgement. Two cooperated and received reduced penalties, while the Virginia candidate publicly defied the process and received the stiffest sanction.

initiative: Joachim Nagel: Already here, not yet everywhere - shaping the economic impact of artificial intelligence
what_happened: Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel delivered a keynote address at the International Economic Symposium in Rome on 21 April 2026, setting out his institutional view on how artificial intelligence is likely to reshape growth, inflation dynamics, and financial stability — the three core variables central banks monitor for policy calibration. He drew an explicit parallel to the slow-burn productivity diffusion seen during electrification, while arguing AI may propagate faster than prior general-purpose technologies, implying a potentially compressed window for policymakers to assess and respond to structural economic shifts.

initiative: Anna Breman: Global shockwaves to Kiwi shores - the impact of the Iran conflict on New Zealand
what_happened: Reserve Bank of New Zealand Governor Anna Breman used a keynote address to Business NZ's CEO Forum in Auckland to formally communicate the central bank's assessment of how the ongoing Iran-related Middle East conflict is transmitting into New Zealand's macroeconomic and financial conditions. She flagged that near-term headline inflation is likely to rise while growth momentum softens, though core inflation has held at 2.4% and medium-term expectations remain anchored—circumstances that constrain but do not preclude further OCR adjustments from the current 2.25% level.

initiative: Vasileios Madouros: Minding the tails - safeguarding resilience of non-bank finance
what_happened: Vasileios Madouros, Deputy Governor of the Central Bank of Ireland, delivered a policy speech to Irish Funds in Dublin on 9 April 2026, setting out the central bank's supervisory posture toward the non-bank financial intermediation (NBFI) sector amid an elevated tail-risk environment. He framed the speech around the structural weight of non-banks — which now hold roughly half of global financial assets — and Ireland's outsized role as a domicile for European fund activity, with approximately €6 trillion in assets under management.

initiative: Dimitar Radev: The current environment is not defined primarily by the baseline scenario. It is defined by the widening range of outcomes around it
what_happened: Bulgarian National Bank Governor Dimitar Radev delivered a formal macroeconomic assessment to diplomatic mission heads in Sofia on 30 March 2026, framing the current conjuncture not as a deviation from a baseline but as a structural widening of the distribution of plausible outcomes — a distinction with direct implications for how central banks and fiscal authorities calibrate policy buffers. Radev disclosed that the ECB Governing Council's March 18–19 meeting specifically modelled Middle East conflict transmission into euro area inflation via energy prices, concluding that short-term effects are manageable but medium-term persistence remains contingent on conflict duration and second-round pass-through.

initiative: ESMA clarifies expectations in the run-up to the launch of EU’s Consolidated Tapes
what_happened: ESMA published a Q&A document addressing the onboarding process for data contributors and operational rules governing the two selected Consolidated Tape Providers — fairCT for bonds and EuroCTP for equities — ahead of their formal authorisation. Trading venues and Authorised Publication Arrangements are reminded of their legal obligation under MiFIR to feed data into the CTs from go-live, and ESMA is pressing these contributors to begin technical engagement with the CTPs now, including agreeing on transmission protocols and completing connectivity and end-to-end testing. CTPs are simultaneously required to implement confidentiality and integrity safeguards for any data received during this pre-authorisation preparatory phase.

initiative: Tabby secures UAE licence
what_happened: Tabby has been granted a Stored Value Facilities (SVF) licence by the Central Bank of the UAE (CBUAE), authorising the company to hold customer funds and introduce a new suite of financial products, including spending accounts, cards and money management tools.

initiative: Moody’s agentic workflows land on AWS Marketplace
what_happened: Moody’s Corporation (NYSE: MCO) today announced that its Moody’s Agentic Solutions (MAS) workflows are now available in AWS Marketplace.

initiative: Richard Byles: Redefining economic leadership - building resilient financial ecosystems in emerging markets
what_happened: Bank of Jamaica Governor Richard Byles addressed Caribbean finance executives at the Caribbean CFO Summit 2026 in Kingston, setting out the central bank's regulatory philosophy and the concrete policy measures underpinning Jamaica's financial stability framework. He disclosed that Jamaica holds a record US$6.8 billion in foreign reserves and confirmed that the central bank has deliberately adopted a calibrated, proportionate adaptation of Basel III standards rather than mechanical full implementation, prioritising liquidity resilience and capital quality improvements suited to the domestic system's structure.

initiative: Dimitar Radev: Economic prospects in an uncertain global environment
what_happened: Bulgarian National Bank Governor Dimitar Radev, speaking at the AmCham Business Breakfast in Sofia on 3 April 2026, laid out the BNB's revised macroeconomic outlook amid energy market disruption stemming from the Middle East conflict, projecting Bulgarian GDP growth of approximately 3.0% for 2026 against an inflation rise to roughly 3.7%, with the bank's internal scenario analysis indicating conditions are tracking closer to its "adverse" rather than baseline path. Radev flagged that reduced fiscal headroom—following several years of drawing down pre-pandemic buffers—limits the state's capacity for broad support measures, shifting a greater share of adjustment onto the private sector in terms of cost management, liquidity discipline, and selective capital allocation.

initiative: CaixaBank collaborates with Flanks to give wealth clients aggregated view of financial assets

initiative: ESMA launches a call for evidence on restricted subscription and private credit ratings
what_happened: ESMA Call for Evidence: Restricted Subscription and Private Credit Ratings ESMA has opened a formal call for evidence seeking market input on restricted subscription and private credit ratings — products where access to the rating output is limited to a defined set of contracting parties rather than disclosed publicly. The initiative is directed at understanding use cases, counterparty characteristics, and whether the analytical rigour and governance controls applied to these non-public ratings are meaningfully comparable to those governing publicly disclosed ratings under the CRA Regulation.

initiative: Michael S Barr: Rural communities - worth the investment
what_happened: Federal Reserve Governor Highlights Rural Capital Access and CDFI Role Speaking at a Washington forum focused on rural investment, Fed Governor Michael Barr drew on field visits to underserved communities — including the Mississippi Delta — to underscore persistent gaps in rural financial access and the institutional responses emerging to address them. He cited a concrete example of a struggling community bank donating its branch building to a CDFI to preserve local financial services, and pointed to New Markets Tax Credits as an effective tool in catalyzing private investment in distressed areas. The remarks signal continued Fed-level attention to community reinvestment frameworks, CDFI capacity, and tax credit mechanisms as policy-relevant instruments for rural economic inclusion.

initiative: BackBase taps Ninth Wave to provide a bridge between banks and client ERP systems
what_happened: Backbase, the leader in AI-native banking technology, and Ninth Wave, a leading provider of secure Open Finance connectivity, announced a strategic partnership today that will enable banks to offer a seamless connection between their business clients’ ERP and accounting systems and their banking data.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement actions with Crédit Agricole S.A. and Crédit Agricole Corporate and Investment Bank, Mega International Commercial Bank Co., Ltd, and the Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
what_happened: The Federal Reserve formally terminated three separate cease-and-desist orders on March 25, 2026, each of which had been in place for between eight and eleven years. The Crédit Agricole order, originally issued in October 2015, targeted the French banking group and its corporate and investment banking arm over compliance deficiencies; the Mega International Commercial Bank order from January 2018 covered the Taiwanese state-linked lender's U.S. branch network across New York, Chicago, and Silicon Valley, where it had faced Bank Secrecy Act and anti-money-laundering failures; and the Goldman Sachs order from May 2018 stemmed from governance and risk-management deficiencies tied to the 1MDB scandal.

initiative: Quantum Bayesian inference: an exploration
what_happened: Researchers from the BIS and affiliated institutions published a working paper examining whether quantum computation can be practically applied to Bayesian inference, a statistical method central to economic forecasting, monetary policy modeling, and risk estimation. The paper presents a proof-of-concept algorithm built using IBM's open-source Qiskit framework in Python, demonstrating posterior sampling by encoding probability distributions into quantum states whose measurements yield draws from a discretized posterior. The authors are candid that the approach does not yet outperform established classical methods such as Markov Chain Monte Carlo or particle filtering, with quantum state preparation remaining a significant bottleneck.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board announces it has made the joint findings with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency required for the OCC to approve a request by Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A., for an exemption under section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act
what_happened: Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A. (Salt Lake City, Utah) sought a regulatory exemption from the affiliate transaction restrictions in Section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act to facilitate an internal corporate reorganization involving its European affiliate, Morgan Stanley Europe SE, based in Frankfurt, Germany. Section 23A normally caps and conditions covered transactions between a bank and its affiliates to limit the risk of a bank subsidizing or being exposed to non-bank entities within the same corporate group. The Fed completed the required joint findings with the OCC — a procedural prerequisite under which the two agencies must concur before the OCC, as Morgan Stanley Bank N.A.'s primary federal regulator, can formally grant the exemption.

initiative: Sound management of third-party risk - Executive Summary
what_happened: BCBS Issues Updated Third-Party Risk Management Principles for Banks The Basel Committee on Banking Supervision published a new set of principles governing how banks must manage risks arising from third-party service provider (TPSP) arrangements, formally superseding the 2005 Joint Forum outsourcing paper and updating the framework to reflect the significantly expanded role vendors, cloud providers, and other external parties now play in core banking operations. The twelve principles follow the full lifecycle of a TPSP engagement — from initial risk assessment and due diligence through contracting, onboarding, ongoing monitoring, and planned or unplanned exit — with board-level accountability explicitly anchored as the governance starting point.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Ally Bank and former employee of Regions Bank
what_happened: The Federal Reserve issued consent prohibition orders against two former bank employees following separate misconduct findings at federally supervised institutions. Lidia Estrada, formerly of Ally Bank's Sandy, Utah operations, was prohibited after falsifying documents in connection with a request for increased compensation, while Brenda Fuson, formerly of Regions Bank in Birmingham, Alabama, faced prohibition for misappropriating customer funds. Both actions were resolved via consent orders, meaning the individuals agreed to the prohibitions without admitting or denying findings, and bar them from future participation in the affairs of any federally insured depository institution.
